original_abstract:
This research investigates the effectiveness of utilizing the textbook "Al-Hilal Fi Fann Al-Lughah Al-Arabiya" to improve the speaking skills (Mahārah al-Kalām) of students at the Ar Riyadh Islamic Boarding School in Palembang. The study was prompted by the observation that the student body is highly heterogeneous—including graduates from non-religious schools and professional employees—while existing instructional materials often relied on archaic or uncommon vocabulary that hindered daily conversational fluency.

Adopting a quantitative experimental methodology, the study involved a sample of students from class 1 "C." Participants were divided into an experimental group, which used the Al-Hilal textbook, and a control group, which followed conventional teaching methods. Data were gathered through observation, interviews, questionnaires, and a pre-test/post-test design. 

Statistical analysis using the t-test revealed a significant improvement in the experimental group, which achieved a t-value of 5.38, surpassing the critical table values at both the 5% and 1% significance levels. In contrast, the control group showed less substantial progress. Qualitative findings from interviews and observations further supported these results, indicating that 86% of students preferred the new material due to its relevant contemporary phrases and practical dialogue structures. The study concludes that "Al-Hilal Fi Fann Al-Lughah Al-Arabiya" is a highly effective tool for developing Arabic speaking proficiency, as it better aligns with the students' communicative needs compared to traditional textbooks.

**Keywords:** Arabic Pedagogy, Speaking Skills, Al-Hilal Textbook, Islamic Boarding School.
humanized_abstract:
This study aims to investigate the effectiveness of the Arabic language textbook entitled “Al-Hilal Fi Fann Al-Lughah Al-Arabiya” in enhancing the student's speaking skill of Mahārah al-Kalām at Ar Riyadh Islamic Boarding School, Palembang. This study was initiated due to several factors such as the heterogeneity of the student population consisted of graduates of the secular schools and working professional and also the old textbooks used at the time often have outdated words that were not frequently used in modern communication.

This study adopts the quantitative experimental research methodology. The population of this study consists of students of class 1 “C.” At the beginning, the students who participated in this study were divided into two main groups. The experimental group used the new textbook “Al-Hilal,” while the control group maintained the regular instructional approach that was adopted in the schools. To support the research instruments, direct observation was used along with verbal interviews with students, parents and teachers. Additionally, the information needed was collected by using observation sheet and (pre-test & post-test) of scientific achievements for the students in both experimental and control groups.

To establish whether there was a statistically significant improvement in the experimental group, a t-test was performed and a t-value of 5.38 was obtained, which is greater than the critical values given in the tables for both 5% and 1% level of significance. The control group did not record the same degree of improvement. The preferred new material was chosen by 86% of the students because they found more modern vocabulary and more suitable phrases and arrangements of language for conversation. The study concludes that 'Al-Hilal Fi Fann Al-Lughah Al-Arabiya' is more valuable for the development of the students' Arabic speaking skills than the current textbooks in use, because it makes the language more relevant to the communicative needs of the students.

**Keywords:** Arabic Pedagogy, Speaking Skills, Al-Hilal Textbook, Islamic Boarding School.
